title: cjk-hostfont
description: The §6.3 HOST-supplied-font contract: the CJK font is passed to the runtime through the `files` map (WasmTeXStubCJK-Regular.ttf, a project file) and selected by a project-relative path with \setCJKmainfont[Path=./]{...} — NOT the bundled fandol. \usepackage{xeCJK} IS a provided-package name in the academic tier, so the §5.4(a) scan preselects + mounts academic before pass 1 (resolution=scan); xeCJK (unlike ctex) loads NO default CJK font, so fandol never enters. Asserted STRUCTURALLY via fontProbe (no pixel comparison, §8): the host font's program is embedded (self-contained PDF), its /BaseFont carries the host font's name, and — the load-bearing negative control — NO fandol font is embedded (absentFonts). The stub font also carries a plain Unicode cmap, so xdvipdfmx writes a ToUnicode CMap and the Chinese round-trips: the recovered text contains the actual characters (你好世界 / 主机字体 / 宿主字体), a direct proof the host font rendered them. Contrast cjk-ctex (bundled fandol, retry path, CJK NOT recoverable). The WasmTeXStubCJK-Regular.ttf fixture is ORIGINAL work (hand-authored rectangular glyphs via fontTools; see conformance/fixtures/), MIT-licensed under the repo — not derived from any third-party font.
